title: Desmodium urarioides
taxonomy: Desmodium urarioides is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. Desmodium urarioides is classified as a legume, which is a type of plant that fixes nitrogen in the soil. This classification is due to its membership in the legume family, specifically within the subfamily Faboideae. As a member of the Fabaceae family, Desmodium urarioides is closely related to other plants such as beans and peas.

The roots of Desmodium urarioides are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. intro: Desmodium urarioides, commonly known as "Gatuka" or "Wild Indigo", is a perennial legume plant native to tropical Africa, characterized by its small yellow or purple flowers and compound leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in African folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Desmodium urarioides include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Desmodium urarioides is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of a powder or tincture to treat various health conditions.
